commit.message:
fix: indexer never writes files, vault-wide block ID uniqueness

Remove block ID assignment from the indexer thread — it was writing
directly to disk via atomic_write(), bypassing the self-write hash
guard and causing false reload prompts.

Block IDs are now assigned only when a file is open in the editor,
via ensure_block_ids() on Insert→Normal transitions. Files without
IDs are indexed as-is.

Also adds vault-wide collision avoidance:
- BloomEditor.known_block_ids cache, refreshed on IndexComplete
- compute_block_id_assignments() accepts optional known IDs set
- New IDs inserted into cache immediately on assignment

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: centralize autosave as debounced timer

Replace 4 scattered autosave_if_dirty() calls with a single
schedule_autosave() at the end of handle_key(). Uses buffer's
existing is_dirty() (version != clean_version) to detect mutations.

- handle_key() = handle_key_inner() + schedule_autosave()
- 300ms debounce: rapid edits/undo/redo coalesce into one save
- Insert mode suppressed (fires on Insert→Normal transition)
- save_page() clears deadline to prevent redundant flush
- Removes undo/redo-specific deadline special cases

fix: suppress false reload prompts during undo/redo

Two-part fix for file watcher echoing our own saves back as external
changes:

1. Content-hash self-write detection: hash every save, suppress watcher
   events whose disk content matches a stored hash (bounded deque of 8).

2. Undo/redo autosave debounce: set 300ms deadline instead of saving
   immediately, coalescing rapid undo sequences into a single write.

commit.message:
feat: undo delta persistence with keyframe support

Store EditDelta (offset, delete_len, insert_text) per undo node instead
of full buffer content. Every 50th node stores a keyframe (full text)
bounding chain length. BFS restore on load reconstructs Ropey structural
sharing. Backward compatible with existing all-content format.

Storage: ~25 MB → ~540 KB for reference vault (20 pages × 500 nodes).

Also adds lab design docs for block ID metadata and undo delta stress
testing (18 correctness scenarios, 5 failure modes).

Update locale strings (#8339)

This PR updates the locale translation strings extracted from source
code.

## Triggered By
Commit: `3fbe7208901ec5992128e85901ecc43d5f368f8b`
Message: fix: Donation fund Active field always shows "Yes" and changes
not retained (#8319)

The Donation Fund editor showed all funds as Active="Yes" regardless of
saved state, and setting a fund to "No" had no effect. Three bugs in
`src/DonationFundEditor.php` were responsible.

## Bugs fixed

- **Reading — `boolval('false') === true`**: The `fun_Active` column
stores the literal strings `'true'`/`'false'` in an
`enum('true','false')` DB column. `boolval()` treats any non-empty
string as truthy, so `'false'` was read back as active.
  ```php
  // Before — always true for stored string 'false'
  $aActiveFields[$row] = boolval($donation->getActive());

  // After
  $aActiveFields[$row] = $donation->getActive() === 'true';
  ```

- **Writing — PHP bool stored into `enum('true','false')` column**:
`setActive()` received a PHP boolean, which Propel casts to `'1'`/`''` —
neither is a valid enum value, so MySQL silently rejected the write.
  ```php
  // Before — stores invalid enum value
  $donation->setActive($_POST[$iFieldID . 'active'] == 1);

  // After
$isActive = isset($_POST[$iFieldID . 'active']) && $_POST[$iFieldID .
'active'] == 1;
  $donation->setActive($isActive ? 'true' : 'false');
  ```

- **Delete action — assignment `=` instead of comparison `==`**: `if
($sAction = 'delete' && strlen($sFund) > 0)` overwrote `$sAction` with a
boolean, meaning any request with a `Fund` query param (regardless of
`Action`) would trigger a deletion.
  ```php
  // Before
  if ($sAction = 'delete' && strlen($sFund) > 0) {
  // After
  if ($sAction == 'delete' && strlen($sFund) > 0) {
  ```

commit.message:
fix: improve pool diagnostics and serialize db verification tests (#159)

## What type of PR is this?

- [x] fix (bug fix)
- [x] test (adding or updating tests)

## Which issue(s) this PR fixes

Fixes #

## What this PR does / why we need it

This PR improves database connection pool diagnostics and fixes flaky DB
verification tests.

On the diagnostics side, it makes main-pool logging more accurate:
- distinguish `empty` (no established connections) from `saturated` (all
established connections busy)
- avoid noisy repeated warnings by logging state transitions and
throttled repeats
- treat idle-drained empty pools as informational, but still emit
warnings/errors when recent real connectivity anomalies occurred
- expose additional pool-health and connection-anomaly metrics in
`/metrics`

On the test side, it serializes `api_db_verify.rs` because those
integration tests share a real database and can interfere with each
other when run concurrently, causing flaky failures unrelated to product
behavior.

### Key changes

- `memoria-storage/src/store.rs`
  - add structured pool health state tracking
- improve monitor logs for `healthy` / `high_utilization` / `saturated`
/ `empty`
- record and surface recent connectivity anomalies such as pool timeout
/ pool closed / IO / TLS / protocol / too-many-connections

- `memoria-api/src/routes/metrics.rs`
  - add main-pool active/state/duration/anomaly metrics
  - add auth-pool active metric

- `memoria-api/tests/api_db_verify.rs`
  - mark DB-sharing integration tests as `#[serial]`

- `memoria-api/Cargo.toml`
  - add `serial_test` dev dependency

### Validation

- `cargo test -p memoria-storage --lib -- --nocapture`
- `cargo test -p memoria-api --test pool_isolation -- --nocapture`
- `cargo test -p memoria-api --test api_db_verify -- --nocapture`
- `make check`

---------

commit.message:
fix: make CustomCmdOverride take precedence over RunnerType command in exec flow (#912)

* Initial plan

* fix: make DockerCmdOverride take precedence over RunnerType args in exec flow

The MCP tests were fundamentally wrong: they created agents without
RunnerType and relied on DockerCmdOverride flowing to session.CustomCmd.
After the legacy Docker run mode was removed (24bade4), the exec flow
is the only code path, but the CustomCmd was treated as a fallback
instead of an override.

Changes:
- DockerAgentRuntime: CustomCmd (DockerCmdOverride) now takes precedence
  over runnerArgs — matching its name as an "override"
- AgentSession.CustomCmd: update comment (no longer "legacy flow" only)
- AssigneeRequest.DockerCmdOverride: update comment about precedence
- MCP tests: set RunnerType=Codex on agents and rely on the override

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/issuepit/issuepit/sessions/0e86d559-07e5-47c9-adba-cc82d1533a19

Co-authored-by: m-s-work <122795841+m-s-work@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: clarify that runner args and DockerCmdOverride are both full docker-exec commands

Both runnerArgs and CustomCmd (DockerCmdOverride) are complete docker-exec
command lists (executable + arguments), NOT the container startup CMD
(which is always "tail -f /dev/null") and NOT additional arguments passed
to the Docker runtime.

- runnerArgs: e.g. ["opencode", "run", "--model", "X", "--format", "json", "TASK"]
- CustomCmd:  e.g. ["sh", "-c", "wget ..."] — replaces the entire runner

Updated all comments/docs to make this distinction explicit:
- DockerAgentRuntime: clarify runnerArgs structure and CustomCmd semantics
- AgentSession.CustomCmd: document as full command list, not just arguments
- AssigneeRequest.DockerCmdOverride: clarify replaces entire invocation
- RetrySessionRequest.DockerCmdOverride: add missing XML doc comment

No UI changes needed — DockerCmdOverride is an API-only diagnostic feature,
intentionally not exposed in the retry dialog.

commit.message:
Fix corrupt SPIR-V shader data in Go Vulkan sample

The vertex shader had a corrupt OpConstantComposite instruction at
offset 84: type ID was 0x1c (28) == result ID 28, creating a circular
reference that spirv-val correctly rejects as invalid SPIR-V.

Root cause: the SPIR-V was copied from the Windows Go Vulkan sample
with data corruption (0x0000001c instead of 0x00000015 as the type ID,
plus several other mismatched IDs).

Fix: recompile both vertex and fragment shaders on Linux using
glslangValidator -V from the same GLSL source code, producing
verified-valid SPIR-V bytecode.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix Go Vulkan pipeline: use static viewport/scissor, no cull mode

- Remove dynamic viewport/scissor states (lavapipe returned VK_ERROR_UNKNOWN -13

- Set MinSampleShading = 1.0 for multisample state
- Provide explicit PViewports and PScissors in viewportState

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix Vulkan: call vk.InitInstance after CreateInstance

vulkan-go requires vk.InitInstance(instance) after CreateInstance to
load per-instance extension function pointers (e.g. surface support,
swapchain, etc.). Without this, extension functions crash with SIGSEGV.

test: fix flaky Ui_HappyPath_CreateTeamAndAddMemberWithRole (#911)

* Initial plan

* test: fix flaky Ui_HappyPath_CreateTeamAndAddMemberWithRole

Apply the same retry pattern used in OrgsPage.CreateOrgAndNavigateAsync
to OrgDetailPage.CreateTeamAsync. After navigating to the org detail
page via the Nuxt client-side router, the Vue @click handler may not
yet be attached when Playwright clicks "New Team", causing the modal to
never open.

The fix: attempt the modal input selector with Short (5s) timeout; if
it times out, delay RetryDelay ms and re-click before using the longer
Navigation (15s) timeout — identical to the established codebase pattern.

Also updates agents.md to document that stable selectors should be
preferred over retry logic, and that retry is a last resort for known
Vue SSR hydration races.
